questions.question:
The octal representation of an integer is (342)_8. If this were to be treated as an eight-bit integer in an 2's complement based computer, its decimal equivalent is
A. 226
B. -98
C. 76
D. -30
questions.to_find: Decimal equivalent of octal (342)_8 when treated as an 8-bit 2's complement integer

understand.plain: The octal number 342 is given. We first convert it to an 8-bit binary number. Since the MSB (most significant bit) turns out to be 1, the number is negative in 2's complement representation. We then find the magnitude by inverting bits and adding 1.

keywords.explain: A way of representing signed integers where the MSB is the sign bit (0=positive, 1=negative). To negate: flip all bits and add 1.
Each octal digit maps to exactly 3 binary digits.

understand.visual_alt: Octal (342)_8 converted to binary 011 100 010 = 11100010 in 8 bits. MSB=1 so negative. 2's complement gives magnitude 00011110 = 30, so value is -30.

given.aim: Find the decimal value of octal (342)_8 treated as an 8-bit 2's complement integer

terms.connects: Convert each octal digit to 3 bits to get 9-bit binary, then take rightmost 8 bits

terms.meaning: Signed representation using 8 bits, MSB is sign bit
terms.connects: If MSB=1, the number is negative; compute magnitude using 2's complement

terms.meaning: Most significant bit indicates sign: 0 positive, 1 negative
terms.connects: Determines whether to interpret value as positive or negative

given.plan: Convert (342)_8 to binary: 011 100 010 = 011100010 (9 bits). Take 8 rightmost bits = 11100010. MSB is 1, so it is negative. Find magnitude: flip bits of 11100010 = 00011101, add 1 = 00011110 = 30. Therefore value is -30.

steps.title: Convert octal to binary
steps.note: The octal number needs only 9 bits but we truncate to 8 bits for an 8-bit system.

steps.title: Interpret as 8-bit 2's complement
steps.formula_raw: If MSB=1: value = -(2's complement of the bit pattern)
steps.apply: 11100010: MSB=1 so negative. Flip bits: 00011101. Add 1: 00011110 = 16+8+4+2 = 30. So the value is -30.

questions.formula_note: 2's complement negation: flip all bits and add 1; MSB=1 indicates a negative number in signed representation.

questions.question:
Suppose the domain set of an attribute consists of signed four digit numbers. What is the percentage of reduction in storage space of this attribute if it is stored as an integer rather than in character form?
A. 80%
B. 20%
C. 60%
D. 40%
questions.to_find: Percentage reduction in storage when a signed 4-digit number is stored as integer instead of character string

understand.plain: A signed 4-digit number like +1234 or -9999 requires 5 characters (sign + 4 digits) if stored as text, each character taking 1 byte. As a binary integer, we need only enough bits to represent values up to 9999 (14 bits = 2 bytes). The reduction is calculated from these storage sizes.

keywords.explain: Each character (digit or sign) is stored as 1 byte (ASCII). A signed 4-digit number needs 5 bytes.
Binary integer uses the minimum bytes needed to represent the range. Signed 4-digit: range -9999 to +9999 needs 14 bits, stored in 2 bytes.

understand.visual_alt: Character form: 5 bytes for a signed 4-digit number. Integer form: 2 bytes. Reduction = (5-2)/5 = 60%.

given.aim: Calculate percentage reduction in storage space: character form vs integer form for signed 4-digit numbers

terms.meaning: Numbers from -9999 to +9999 with a sign
terms.connects: Determines the storage needed in both formats

terms.meaning: Binary representation; range 0-9999 needs ceil(log_2(10000)) = 14 bits = 2 bytes
terms.example: 9999 in binary = 10011100001111 (14 bits), fits in 2 bytes

given.plan: Character storage for signed 4-digit number = 5 bytes (sign + 4 digits). Integer storage = 2 bytes (since 9999 < 2^14, 2 bytes = 16 bits suffice). Percentage reduction = (5-2)/5 * 100 = 60%.

steps.title: Calculate character storage
steps.formula_raw: Bytes = (number of characters) x 1 byte each
steps.apply: A signed 4-digit number has: 1 sign character + 4 digit characters = 5 characters = 5 bytes.
steps.note: Each ASCII character takes exactly 1 byte.

steps.title: Calculate integer storage
steps.apply: Max value = 9999. log_2(9999) ~ 13.3, so 14 bits for magnitude. With sign: 15 bits, fits in 2 bytes (16 bits). Integer storage = 2 bytes.
steps.note: 2 bytes (16 bits) can represent signed values from -32768 to +32767, which covers -9999 to +9999.

steps.title: Calculate percentage reduction
steps.note: The integer form uses 60% less storage than the character form.

questions.formula_note: Storage comparison: character form requires 1 byte per character (5 bytes for signed 4-digit), integer form requires 2 bytes; reduction = (5-2)/5 * 100 = 60%.

questions.question:
Zero has two representations in
A. Sign-magnitude
B. 1's complement
C. 2's complement
D. None of the above
questions.to_find: Which number representation systems have two representations for zero

understand.plain: In some number systems, zero can be represented in two different ways. Sign-magnitude has +0 (00000000) and -0 (10000000). 1's complement also has +0 (00000000) and -0 (11111111). In 2's complement, zero has only one representation (00000000) because the -0 pattern wraps around to represent -128 instead.

keywords.explain: MSB is the sign bit (0=positive, 1=negative), remaining bits are magnitude. +0 is 00000000, -0 is 10000000.
Negative numbers formed by flipping all bits. +0 = 00000000, -0 = 11111111.
Negative numbers by flipping bits and adding 1. Zero has only one form: 00000000. 10000000 = -128, not -0.

understand.visual_svg: <svg width='480' height='180' viewBox='0 0 480 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='10' y='10' width='140' height='60' class='hi' rx='6'/><text x='80' y='32' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>Sign-Magnitude</text><text x='80' y='52' class='sub mut' text-anchor='middle'>+0: 00000000</text><text x='80' y='68' class='sub mut' text-anchor='middle'>-0: 10000000</text><rect x='170' y='10' width='140' height='60' class='hi' rx='6'/><text x='240' y='32' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>1's Complement</text><text x='240' y='52' class='sub mut' text-anchor='middle'>+0: 00000000</text><text x='240' y='68' class='sub mut' text-anchor='middle'>-0: 11111111</text><rect x='330' y='10' width='140' height='60' class='bg' rx='6'/><text x='400' y='32' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>2's Complement</text><text x='400' y='52' class='sub mut' text-anchor='middle'>+0: 00000000</text><text x='400' y='68' class='sub mut' text-anchor='middle'>(only one zero)</text><text x='80' y='105' class='tag ok' text-anchor='middle'>Two zeros</text><text x='240' y='105' class='tag ok' text-anchor='middle'>Two zeros</text><text x='400' y='105' class='tag no' text-anchor='middle'>One zero</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Sign-magnitude and 1's complement both have two representations for zero (+0 and -0). 2's complement has only one zero.

given.aim: Identify which number representation systems have two representations of zero

terms.meaning: +0 = 0000...0 and -0 = 1000...0 are both valid representations of zero
terms.connects: Two distinct bit patterns represent zero
Two distinct bit patterns represent zero

terms.meaning: Only 0000...0 represents zero; the pattern 1000...0 represents the most negative number (-2^(n-1))
terms.connects: Unique representation for zero, no ambiguity

given.plan: Check each representation: sign-magnitude and 1's complement both have +0 and -0 as distinct bit patterns. In 2's complement, trying to form -0 gives 00000000 again (wraps around), so only one zero exists.

steps.title: Check sign-magnitude
steps.apply: In sign-magnitude, +0 = 00000000 and -0 = 10000000. These are two different bit patterns, both representing zero.
steps.note: Sign-magnitude has two zeros, causing arithmetic complications.

steps.title: Check 1's complement
steps.apply: +0 = 00000000. -0 = flip all bits = 11111111. Two distinct patterns for zero.
steps.note: End-around carry in 1's complement arithmetic handles this ambiguity.

steps.title: Check 2's complement
steps.apply: +0 = 00000000. To get -0: flip = 11111111, add 1 = 100000000, but the carry out drops off in 8 bits = 00000000. So -0 = +0 in 2's complement.
steps.note: 2's complement has a unique zero, which is why it is universally preferred.

solution.result: Zero has two representations in Sign-magnitude (A) and 1's complement (B)

questions.formula_note: In sign-magnitude and 1's complement, both +0 and -0 exist as distinct bit patterns. In 2's complement, -0 wraps to +0, giving a unique zero.

questions.question:
The number 43 in 2's complement representation is
A. 01010101
B. 11010101
C. 00101011
D. 10101011

understand.plain: The question asks for the 8-bit binary representation of the number 43. Since 43 is a positive number, its 2's complement representation is simply its binary form with a 0 in the MSB (sign bit). We convert 43 to binary directly.

keywords.explain: For positive numbers, 2's complement is the same as standard binary representation with MSB=0.
Divide repeatedly by 2 and collect remainders, or recognize powers of 2.

understand.visual_alt: 43 = 32+8+2+1 = 00101011 in binary. Since 43 is positive, its 8-bit 2's complement is 00101011.

terms.meaning: Represented identically to unsigned binary, with MSB=0
Exactly 8 binary digits with leading zeros if needed
terms.connects: Determines the format of the answer

given.plan: Since 43 is positive, convert 43 to binary directly. 43 = 32+8+2+1 = 2^5+2^3+2^1+2^0 = 00101011. For 8-bit 2's complement, MSB must be 0 (positive), giving 00101011.

steps.title: Convert 43 to binary
steps.note: 43 is positive so no 2's complement negation is needed.

steps.title: Verify 8-bit 2's complement form
steps.formula_raw: For positive n: 2's complement = binary with leading 0
steps.apply: 00101011: MSB=0 (positive), and value = 32+8+2+1 = 43. This matches option A: 00101011.
steps.note: Checking options: A=01010101=85 (not 43), B=11010101=-43 (negative), C=00101011=32+8+2+1=43 (correct), D=10101011=-85 (negative). Option C matches.

questions.formula_note: Positive numbers have identical representation in unsigned binary and 2's complement; just convert to binary.

questions.question:
Consider the values of A = 2.0 x 10^8, B = -2.0 x 10^8, C = 1.0, and the sequence
X := A + B
Y := A + C
X := X + C
Y := Y + B
executed on a computer where floating point numbers are represented with 32 bits. The values for X and Y will be
A. X = 1.0, Y = 1.0
B. X = 1.0, Y = 0.0
C. X = 0.0, Y = 1.0
D. X = 0.0, Y = 0.0
questions.to_find: Final values of X and Y after the sequence of floating-point operations

understand.plain: This problem demonstrates floating-point precision issues. When we add a very large number (2x10^8) and a very small number (1.0), the small number may be lost due to limited precision. A 32-bit float has only about 7 significant decimal digits. 2.0x10^8 has 9 digits, so adding 1.0 to it loses the 1.0 due to rounding.

keywords.explain: 32-bit IEEE 754 float has 23-bit mantissa giving ~7 significant decimal digits. Numbers differing by more than 10^7 in magnitude cannot both be represented exactly.
When two nearly equal large numbers are subtracted, the result has few significant digits.

understand.visual_alt: X path: A+B = 2e8 + (-2e8) = 0.0 exactly, then X = 0.0+1.0 = 1.0. Y path: A+C = 2e8+1 = 2e8 (1.0 lost in 32-bit), then Y = 2e8+(-2e8) = 0.0. So X=1.0, Y=0.0.

given.aim: Determine final values of X and Y considering 32-bit floating point precision limitations

terms.meaning: A large positive floating point number, 200,000,000
terms.connects: Exceeds 32-bit float precision when added to small numbers

terms.example: Much smaller than A and B
terms.connects: Lost in precision when added to A or B directly

terms.meaning: About 7 significant decimal digits; 2x10^8 + 1 rounds back to 2x10^8
terms.example: 2x10^8 has 9 significant digits; 1.0 is at position 10^0, which is beyond the 7-digit precision
terms.connects: Causes Y path to lose C when computing A+C

given.plan: Trace X and Y separately. X: A+B = 2e8+(-2e8) = 0.0 (exact cancellation), then X+C = 0.0+1.0 = 1.0. Y: A+C = 2e8+1 ~= 2e8 (1.0 lost, precision exceeded), then Y+B = 2e8+(-2e8) = 0.0.

steps.title: Compute X = A + B, then X = X + C
steps.apply: Step 1: X = A + B = 2.0x10^8 + (-2.0x10^8) = 0.0 (exact, same exponent, opposite signs cancel perfectly). Step 2: X = X + C = 0.0 + 1.0 = 1.0.
steps.note: A and B have the same magnitude and opposite signs; exact cancellation gives 0.0 regardless of precision.

steps.title: Compute Y = A + C, then Y = Y + B
steps.apply: Step 1: Y = A + C = 2.0x10^8 + 1.0. In 32-bit float (~7 digits), 200000000 + 1 = 200000001 has 9 significant digits — beyond precision. Result rounds to 2.0x10^8. So Y = 2.0x10^8. Step 2: Y = Y + B = 2.0x10^8 + (-2.0x10^8) = 0.0.
steps.note: The 1.0 is swamped by the large magnitude of A and is lost in rounding, so Y ends up as 0.0.

questions.formula_note: Floating-point arithmetic is not associative due to limited precision; adding a small number to a large one can lose the small number (absorption error).

questions.question:
The 2's complement representation of (-539)_10 in hexadecimal is
A. ABD
B. DBC
C. DE5
D. 9ET

understand.plain: To find the 2's complement of -539, we first convert 539 to binary, then flip all bits and add 1. The result is then grouped into 4-bit nibbles and converted to hex. We use a 12-bit or convenient representation large enough to hold 539.

keywords.explain: To represent -N: convert N to binary, invert all bits, add 1.
Base-16 number system with digits 0-9 and A-F. Each hex digit represents 4 binary bits.

given.aim: Find the 2's complement hexadecimal representation of -539

terms.meaning: Flip every 0 to 1 and every 1 to 0
Add binary 1 to the inverted result

given.plan: Convert 539 to binary. Use 12-bit representation: 001000011011. Invert all bits: 110111100100. Add 1: 110111100101. Group in 4-bit nibbles: 1101 1110 0101 = DE5.

steps.title: Convert 539 to binary (12-bit)
steps.note: Use 12 bits (multiple of 4) for easy hex conversion.

steps.title: Convert to hexadecimal
steps.formula_raw: Group 4 bits from right to left -> hex digit
steps.note: Verification: DE5 in decimal = 13*256 + 14*16 + 5 = 3328+224+5 = 3557. As 12-bit signed: 3557 - 4096 = -539. Correct!

questions.formula_note: 2's complement of N: invert all bits of N then add 1; group resulting bits into nibbles for hex conversion.
